title: Worst Universities in the UK #university #students #studentlife #ranking #knowledge #uni #facts
body:
In 2012 I attended north middlesex uni in London.
It was one of the most oddest years of my life.
My entire class were dumb, silent and didn't know how to answer questions which made my morale feel low.
The teachers didn't connect well at all with us and couldn't teach properly, they never answered their emails. Ever.

title: PENN STATE PROS AND CONS #shorts #college #review #collegelife #school #schoollife
body:
As someone who lived in the area as a non-student, there is lots of great restaurants too! And a lot of them are only open when school is in session.

Con - State College, the town were Penn State is located can be quite stressful to navigate sometimes, especially when school is in. The streets are quite narrow and there can be A LOT of vehicular traffic. Also, don't be surprised if people walk out in front of you in the streets while you're trying drive. Evennid you're the one that has the right-of-way. It happens A LOT.

When students are in, it almost has a city feel to it. At least downtown does.
author: @TMIATC

title: Touring Every College In Boston So You Don't Have To | (Harvard, MIT, BU, Northeastern, etc.)
body:
@ashtonherndon In general it's pretty decent review video. I do think your verbal reviews are not consistent with the actual rating your gave for some of the schools.

For example you said BU has everything you need at an arms length away; but you gave it a 7 for convenience. In your words .. "BU is the most amenity having campus on our list" ... but you gave it an 8 for amenities. With the quote alone, it should have been the only 10 on the list.

For Tufs, you called it secluded, no traditional chains that your normally see and "there is nothing to do outside of campus". but you gave it an 8 for convenience, higher than BU!?!.

According to BU and Tufs, a rating of 9 for convenience for MIT is very generous almost preferential.

Last thing...I love your energy but your speech cadence is fast, quick and a blur. Which is consistent with how you edit. I think if the viewers has to pause the video thats a reflections of the editing. Keep the same energy; but try not to make it sound and feel rushed.

I took the time to comment because I truly liked your video...Keep up the good work.
author: @neveoharrison7032

title: pros & cons | northeastern university
body: Hi! I'm in the Boston NUin 2020 program and super excited to start. Thank you for this video :) This year is definitely crazy. Only Ireland and Boston are left. I believe NUin also accounts for students that have scores and GPAs they want to leave out to have higher percentages and move the school up the ranks. This year we'll be living in the Westin Hotel. There are 1000 students in Boston and a few hundred in Ireland, I believe.
author: @anyaovch

title: pros & cons | northeastern university
body: Thank you so much for this video! I'm a senior in highschool and I fell in love with NU. However, I'm a prospective CSSH student (political science and criminal justice or political science and international affairs), and I come from a tiny town where we don't have many AP classes available, or many huge opportunities to put on college apps. There are 7 APs offered at my school and I will have taken 4 of them by the time I graduate. I got 5s on both the ones I have already taken. I am class president, in NHS, Student Council, and leader of two other clubs. I'm not submitting my SAT scores because they aren't very good, and my school doesn't do GPA or rankings (we are proficiency based), but I would be at the top of my class with the equivalent of something over a 4.0. I volunteer and have work experience. My essay is pretty good and I'm going to apply ED in November so I still have some time to perfect it, but do you think Humanities majors would have an easier time getting into NU than STEM/Business majors? I know my stats aren't the best but I really really want to go to NU. I also don't mind as much if the programs for humanities aren't as funded, I looked at the coop posters for political science + criminal justice and I was almost foaming at the mouth because I want to go here so badly. Sorry for the excessively long comment, but your video was so informative and wanted to see if you could answer my question :)
author: @genevieve2128

title: week in my life as a freshman at columbia university | exploring nyc, living alone
body: i developed  severe panic attacks in October 2022. i found hanging out with someone helped reduce the chance of panic attack. but, problem is i could only depend on that person twice. again, i found myself by myself again and learned i would be responsible to dealing with my panic attacks and cannot rely on the presence of another to reduce the likelihood of it occuring. So, in January I restructured my lifestyle with exercise and deep-breathing, knowing if i did not get my panic attacks under control, i would develop agoraphobia and would not leave my house. my classes take attendance & so there was no option to not become stronger and embrace my independence. i guess we are our own heroes. <3 thanks for sharing your story at the end.
author: @Jyeah89

author: @urmidhamnaskar2497

title: A DAY IN MY LIFE AS A COSMETIC CHEMIST #cosmeticchemist #cosmeticscience
body:
📣Dear Students and parents. Attention please📣📢

If you are very sure of getting only into the cosmetics industry, a BSc in Cosmetic Science and an MTech in Cosmetic Technology or a BTech in Cosmetic Technology and MSc in Cosmetic Science is the best to go with, you can also consider pursuing BSc and MSc in Cosmetic Science.

If you are not sure, then a BSc in Chemistry and a MSc in Cosmetic Science is good to go with, but be prepared for getting into teaching job if you couldn't pursue MSc in Cosmetic Science and get into cosmetic industry, other wise BPharm or BPharm (Ayurveda) and MSc in Cosmetic Science or MTech in Cosmetic Technology would be a safer option.
